_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
ReCFIT (Renewables, Climate and Future Industries Tasmania) is a division of the Department of State Growth that functions as Tasmania's primary regulatory and policy authority for climate change, renewable energy, and future industries. It does not itself impose direct compliance obligations on commercial freight operators analogous to a customs or aviation regulator; instead, it establishes the legislative and programmatic framework within which all Tasmanian businesses — including freight forwarders — must operate.

The workspace, as a freight forwarder with Tasmanian operational footprint (air, road, and ocean movements into or out of the island state), sits in the compliance chain primarily as a *downstream obligated business* under the *Climate Change (State Action) Act 2008* (as amended November 2022). The Act's net-zero-by-2030 legislative target, and the sector-based Emissions Reduction and Resilience Plans (ERRPs), create a policy environment in which major Tasmanian emitters — including heavy transport operators — are expected to reduce emissions. The Transport ERRP 2024-29 explicitly targets decarbonisation of the heavy transport fleet via electric vehicles, hydrogen fuel cells, and renewable hydrocarbon fuels. Freight forwarders operating heavy road vehicles in Tasmania are named-sector participants in this policy framework even if not yet subject to mandatory performance standards.

The workspace is also a potential *grant-eligible business* under multiple ReCFIT programs (Climate Change Business Innovation Grants, PowerSmart for Small Business, Business Energy Efficiency Scheme), and a potential *supply chain participant* in the Renewable Energy Services Hub at Bell Bay — relevant if the workspace has cargo customers in the renewable energy project pipeline (wind, solar, hydrogen).

Role placement requiring legal confirmation: Whether the workspace qualifies as a 'major emitter' subject to the Australian Government's Safeguard Mechanism (referenced in ReCFIT's Energy ERRP) and thus whether any Tasmanian-specific reporting obligations layer on top of national reporting requirements. Additionally, the precise obligations that may arise from forthcoming amendments to the *Climate Change (State Action) Act 2008* following the December 2025 independent review are not yet enacted and must be monitored.

*Source: ReCFIT — What is ReCFIT?, Department of State Growth, current. https://www.recfit.tas.gov.au/what_is_recfit; Climate Change (State Action) Act 2008 — Tasmania's climate change legislation, ReCFIT, updated post-November 2022. https://www.recfit.tas.gov.au/what_is_recfit/climate_change/climate_change_legislation; Transport Emissions Reduction and Resilience Plan 2024-29, ReCFIT/Department of State Growth, 2024. https://www.recfit.tas.gov.au/policies_strategies_plans/emissions_reduction/transport/transport_emissions_reduction_and_resilience_plan_2024-29*
